About Fred

Fred Carden is an evaluation specialist with 30+ years of experience in development research evaluation and methodology. His work emphasizes how evidence is used — from policy influence to organization‑wide learning.

Highlights

  • Founded Using Evidence Inc. (2015) to improve the use of research and evaluative evidence in decision making.
  • Leadership & advisory: Board — Partnership for Economic Policy; Advisor — Global Development Network, Doing Research.
  • Editorial boards: American Journal of Evaluation, Journal of Evidence & Policy, and New Directions for Evaluation.
  • Education: PhD (Université de Montréal, 1990); Fellowship in Sustainability Science (Harvard, 2008).
  • Based in Ottawa, Canada.

Current work

Uptake of agricultural research

National‑level uptake across several African countries.

Implementation research

Design and evaluation of implementation research initiatives.

Fourth Industrial Revolution

Exploring implications in middle‑income countries.

Learning systems

Embedding evaluative thinking into organizations.
